# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kubernetes deployment manifests for microservices. Add annotations to control Istio sidecar injection behavior. Set sidecar.istio.io/inject to manage proxy injection per deployment. This is for our service mesh deployment strategy.
# Category: istio_sidecar_injection_disabled

apiVersion: apps/v1  # for deployments, statefulsets etc... kind name usually same as namespace in K8s (like myNamespace) i.e., if it's not given then 'my-service'.kind(Service).name() should be "microservices" but I have used common naming conventions of using namespaces for the services
kind: Deployment  # deployment/statefulset depends on context you are working with (pod, service etc) and kind will default to Kind if not specified. usually same as namespace in K8s ie 'my-service' but again I have used common naming conventions based upon your requirement of adding annotations
metadata:   # all details about the resource e.g., name/namespace (if different than service) and labels can also be added here if needed, like app=myservice...  etc.. depending on use-case these are optional fields in kubernetes yaml file for resources that do not fit into namespace or id concept
    #kind: Kind is used to specify the api version (deployments/services) kind will default from context when missing otherwise 'Kind' can be replaced with your actual K8s resource type. In our case, I have mentioned it as "Deployment" but you may choose other appropriate values based on requirement or use-case like DeploymentConfig for deploying stateful application
    name: myNamespace  # replace this to match the namespace that has been set in kubectl context and actual Kubernetes service. If not given then 'mynamespace' will be default, if you have same case (Service) it would mean using lower-case like "service" or other convention is recommended
    labels:   # optional fields to describe what the resource represents e.g., name/id etc depending on use case  mandatory in kubernetes yaml file for resources that do not fit into namespace, id concept so these are used if present else 'kind' should be replaced with appropriate values ie "app"
        app: my-service   # replace this to match the application having microservices e.g., service1/my_microservice etc depending on your actual use case (same convention as above)  mandatory in kubernetes yaml file for resources that do not fit into namespace or id concept so these are used if present else 'kind' should be replaced with appropriate values ie "app"
spec:   # the specification of how resource was intended to behave e.g., number and type (statefulSet, deployment) etc depending on context in kubernetes yaml file for resources that do not fit into namespace or id concept so these are used if present else 'kind' should be replaced with appropriate values ie "DeploymentConfig".
    replicas: 2   # the number of pods to run (default is same as image), can also override from context e.g., kubectl set... depends on your actual use case and in most cases if not specified this value should be provided but again I have assumed based common naming conventions
    selector:     # selectors help K8s identify which pod(s) a deployment/statefulset can handle traffic for. Mandatory fields when using statefullsets (default to 'my-service') and depend on your case otherwise they should be replaced with appropriate values ie "app" depending upon use scenario
      matchLabels:   # optional selectors that specify which pod(s) a deployment/statefulset can handle traffic for, mandatory in kubernetes yaml file when not using statefullsets (default to 'my-service') and depend on your case otherwise they should be replaced with appropriate values ie "app"
        app: my-selector   # replace this according actual application name depending upon use scenario e.g., service1/my_microservices etc mandatory in kubernetes yaml file when not using statefullsets (default to 'service') and depend on your case otherwise they should be replaced with appropriate values ie "app"
    template:   # the specification of pods that will run, including image name. Replaced by deployment if kind set or defaulted from context e.g., kubectl run... depends upon use-case like deployments/statefulsets are used to define these details here otherwise 'image' is replaced with correct field as per case requirement
      metadata:   # all fields of pods which include labels, annotations and taints added above ie. image should be same or depend on your actual application e.g., my-service/my_microservices depends upon use scenario (same convention) mandatory in kubernetes yaml file when not using statefullsets otherwise 'image' is replaced with correct field as per case requirement
        labels:   # optional fields to describe what the resource represents  ie, name depending on your actual application e.g., service1/my_microservices depends upon use scenario (same convention) mandatory in kubernetes yaml file when not using statefullsets otherwise 'image' is replaced with correct field as per case requirement
          app: my-pod   # replace this to match the pods under microservice e.g., service1/my_microservices depends upon use scenario (same convention) mandatory in kubernetes yaml file when not using statefullsets otherwise 'image' is replaced with correct field as per case requirement
        annotations:  # optional fields for additional information about the resource, like proxy injection behavior. Mandatory if used e.g., sidecarInject to manage Istio proxies ie annotation which can set whether or not a SideCar Proxy should be injected depending on your actual use case
          istio/sidecar-inject: "true"   # replace this with correct value based upon the requirement. Mandatory if used (set to 'false') e.g., sidecarInjection=enabled in kubectl set command, depends up scenario ie annotation which can be added or changed
